repository_ssim: St. Catherine's Monastery of the Sinai
titles_tesim: John of Dailam and his miracles in Mosul; Maximus and Dometius
Martyrdom of Qāyātīs, Aurāūs, and Cornelius the Centurion
contents_tesim: John of Dailam and his miracles in Mosul during the reign of the Omaiyads, and Maximus and Dometius the children of King Leontius
John of Dailam and his miracles in Mosul; Maximus and Dometius
Martyrdom of Qāyātīs, Aurāūs, and Cornelius the Centurion
Martyrdom of Qāyātīs, the woman-slave of King Costa of Alexandria, of Aurāūs and his seven relatives in Egypt, and of Cornelius the Centurion, the first of the Romans to embrace Christianity
paracontent_tesim: Here and in Sinai Arabic 531, the scribe Agathon name seems to have been written by a later hand, where the original text is erased.
The “+” is the same as given in the manuscript, indicating a marginal note.

وكان الفراغ من نسخة في اليوم الخامس في شهر آذار لسنة ستة الاف وسبعمائه أحد وأربعين لأبينا آدم عليه السلم الموافق لسنة الهجرة ستة وثلاثين وستمائة  وكتب العبد الخاطئ (+) المسمى أغاتون بالاسم راهب بطور سيناء الجبل المقدس / فصح فهو يسأل كلمن وفق عليه وقرأ فيه يسامح له في الغلط والزلل وترحم عليه وعلى والديه رحم الله من فعل ذلك وغفر له والديه ولبني المعمودية أجمعين آمين  آمين

Martyrdom of Qāyātīs, the woman-slave of King Costa of Alexandria, of Aurāūs and his seven relatives in Egypt, and of Cornelius the Centurion, the first of the Romans to embrace Christianity
The “+” is the same as given in the manuscript, indicating a marginal note.
